entities.name: PEARL MILLET
entities.description: A cereal crop being genetically improved for agronomic traits, stress tolerance, yield, and nutritional quality

entities.name: AGRONOMIC TRAITS
entities.description: General characteristics of a crop that are targets for genetic improvement

entities.name: STRESS TOLERANCE
entities.description: The ability of a plant to withstand adverse environmental conditions

entities.name: YIELD
entities.description: The amount of agricultural production per unit area

entities.name: NUTRITIONAL QUALITY
entities.description: The nutrient composition and value of the crop

entities.name: GENOTYPING
entities.description: The process of determining the genetic makeup of an organism

entities.name: HIGH THROUGHPUT MARKERS
entities.description: Markers that allow for the rapid analysis of many genetic loci

entities.name: GENETIC DIVERSITY
entities.description: The total number of genetic characteristics in the genetic makeup of a species

entities.name: POPULATION STRUCTURE
entities.description: The distribution of genetic variation within and between populations

entities.name: LINKAGE DISEQUILIBRIUM
entities.description: The non-random association of alleles at different loci

entities.name: MARKER-TRAIT ASSOCIATION STUDIES
entities.description: Studies that link specific genetic markers to observable traits

entities.name: GENOMIC-ASSISTED BREEDING
entities.description: Breeding strategies that use genomic information to select for desired traits

entities.name: GENOTYPING-BY-SEQUENCING
entities.description: A high-throughput genotyping method that uses restriction enzymes and sequencing

entities.name: AFRICA
entities.description: Geographic region from which pearl millet landraces and varieties were sourced

entities.name: INDIA
entities.description: Geographic region from which pearl millet landraces and varieties were sourced

entities.name: SINGLE NUCLEOTIDE POLYMORPHISM
entities.description: A variation in a single nucleotide that can serve as a genetic marker

entities.name: REFERENCE GENOME
entities.description: A representative genome sequence used as a standard for mapping

entities.name: POPULATION STRUCTURE ANALYSIS
entities.description: The process of analyzing genetic data to identify subpopulations

entities.name: GENETIC DISTANCE
entities.description: A measure of the genetic divergence between individuals or populations

entities.name: LD DECAY
entities.description: The rate at which linkage disequilibrium decreases as the distance between markers increases

entities.name: POPULATION GENOMIC ANALYSIS
entities.description: The study of the genome of a population to understand its evolution and structure

entities.name: HETEROTIC GROUPS
entities.description: Groups of genetically diverse individuals that produce superior offspring when crossed

entities.name: HYBRID BREEDING
entities.description: The process of crossing two different lines to create a hybrid with superior traits

entities.name: TRAIT MAPPING
entities.description: The process of identifying the genomic location of a trait

entities.name: HAPLOTYPE BLOCKS
entities.description: Regions of the genome where alleles are inherited together
